perturbation.original_text: A. Guarantors shall pay or cause to be paid to Franchisor all monies payable by Master Franchisee under the Master Franchise Agreement on the days and times and in the manner therein appointed for payment thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: A. Guarantors shall endeavor to pay or cause to be paid to Franchisor all monies payable by Master Franchisee under the Master Franchise Agreement on the days and times and in the manner therein appointed for payment thereof, provided that the Master Franchisee has sufficient funds.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall pay' to 'shall endeavor to pay, provided that the Master Franchisee has sufficient funds', the obligation of the Guarantors becomes conditional. This contradicts section B, which unconditionally guarantees full performance, creating uncertainty about the extent of the guarantee.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. Guarantors shall unconditionally guarantee full performance and discharge by Master Franchisee of all the obligations of Master Franchisee under the Master Franchise Agreement at the times and in the manner therein provided.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Guarantors 'shall pay' unconditionally. The changed text introduces a condition ('endeavor to pay, provided that the Master Franchisee has sufficient funds'). This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which states the Guarantors 'shall unconditionally guarantee full performance'. The changed text makes the guarantee conditional, directly conflicting with the requirement of an unconditional guarantee, thus creating an in-text contradiction under the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: D. Franchisor shall not be obligated to proceed against Master Franchisee or exhaust any security from Master Franchisee or pursue or exhaust any remedy, including any legal or equitable relief against Master Franchisee, before proceeding to enforce the obligations of the Guarantors under this Guarantee, and the enforcement of such obligations may take place before, after, or contemporaneously with, enforcement of any debt or obligation of Master Franchisee under the Master Franchise Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: D. Franchisor shall attempt to proceed against Master Franchisee and exhaust available remedies, but will not be required to if doing so would cause undue delay or hardship, before enforcing the obligations of the Guarantors under this Guarantee, and the enforcement of such obligations should ideally take place after enforcement of any debt or obligation of Master Franchisee under the Master Franchise Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'shall not be obligated' to 'shall attempt' introduces ambiguity regarding the Franchisor's recourse against the Master Franchisee. Additionally, the added clause 'but will not be required to if doing so would cause undue delay or hardship,' creates room for interpretation of what constitutes 'undue delay or hardship.' Also it introduces a contradiction with the rest of the agreement where it says that 'Franchisor shall not be obligated'
perturbation.contradicted_text: E. Without affecting the Guarantors' obligations under this Guarantee, Franchisor, without notice to the Guarantors, may extend, modify, or release any indebtedness or obligation of Master Franchisee, or settle, adjust, or compromise any claims against Master Franchisee. Guarantors waive notice of amendment of the Master Franchise Agreement and notice of demand for payment or performance by Master Franchisee.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the Franchisor 'shall not be obligated' to proceed against the Master Franchisee before pursuing the Guarantors. The changed text states that the Franchisor 'shall attempt to proceed' but is not required to if it would cause undue delay or hardship, and 'should ideally' take place after enforcement of any debt. This creates ambiguity and directly contradicts the original lack of obligation and introduces subjectivity with 'undue delay or hardship'. The contradicted text states that Franchisor, without notice to the Guarantors, may extend, modify, or release any indebtedness or obligation of Master Franchisee. The change introduces a contradiction because it now implies that Franchisor has to first attempt to proceed against the Master Franchisee, where earlier it could modify or release any obligations without notice.

perturbation.original_text: H. This Guarantee shall be interpreted and construed under the laws of the state in which Franchisor has its principal place of business at the time the action is initiated. In the event of any conflict of law, the laws of such state shall prevail, without regard to the application of such state's conflict of law rules. If, however, any provision of this Guarantee would not be enforceable under the laws of the state in which Franchisor has its principal place of business at the time the action is initiated, and if the Master Franchisee's franchised business is located outside of such state and such provision would be enforceable under the laws of the state in which the Master Franchisee's franchised business is located, then such provision shall be interpreted and construed under the laws of that state.
perturbation.changed_text: H. This Guarantee shall be interpreted and construed under the laws of the state in which the Master Franchisee is incorporated. In the event of any conflict of law, the laws of the state in which the Guarantor resides shall prevail. If, however, any provision of this Guarantee would not be enforceable under the laws of the state in which the Guarantor resides, and if the Master Franchisee's franchised business is located outside of such state and such provision would be enforceable under the laws of the state in which the Master Franchisee's franchised business is located, then such provision shall be interpreted and construed under the laws of that state.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces conflicting legal standards for interpreting the guarantee. Originally, the law of the Franchisor's principal place of business governed. Now, the primary rule defaults to the law of the Master Franchisee's incorporation, creating an ambiguity regarding which jurisdiction governs the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: H. This Guarantee shall be interpreted and construed under the laws of the state in which the Master Franchisee is incorporated. In the event of any conflict of law, the laws of the state in which the Guarantor resides shall prevail. If, however, any provision of this Guarantee would not be enforceable under the laws of the state in which the Guarantor resides, and if the Master Franchisee's franchised business is located outside of such state and such provision would be enforceable under the laws of the state in which the Master Franchisee's franchised business is located, then such provision shall be interpreted and construed under the laws of that state. Any action brought to enforce or interpret this Guarantee in any court, whether federal or state, shall be brought within the county and state in which Franchisor has its principal place of business at the time the action is initiated, and Guarantors hereby waive all questions of personal jurisdiction or venue for the purpose of carrying out this provision.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the governing law from the Franchisor's principal place of business to the Master Franchisee's incorporation. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which uses the same altered language but then specifies venue as the Franchisor's principal place of business. These clauses are incompatible because they establish conflicting jurisdictions for interpreting the guarantee and resolving disputes.
